title: String Manipulation Master
description:
Practice essential string operations by implementing four utility functions.

1. `format_name(first, last)` — Returns the name in "Last, First" format
2. `extract_initials(name)` — Returns initials with dots (e.g., "John Doe" → "J.D.")
3. `count_vowels(text)` — Returns the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u) in the text (case-insensitive)
4. `reverse_words(sentence)` — Returns the sentence with word order reversed

Examples:
  format_name("John", "Doe") → "Doe, John"
  extract_initials("John Doe") → "J.D."
  count_vowels("Hello World") → 3
  reverse_words("hello world") → "world hello"

hints: Use f-strings to combine last and first name with a comma
Use .split() to break a name into parts, then take [0] of each
Use a loop or sum() with a generator to count vowels
Use .split() to get words, [::-1] to reverse, and ' '.join() to rejoin

testCases.description: extract_initials should return initials with dots
count_vowels should count all vowels case-insensitively
